validation.

commit.message:
fix(feed): canonicalize cache tags + OpenAI manage_stock (#852)

* fix(feed): canonicalize cache tags to merchant UUID + OpenAI manage_stock

- Restructure both feed routes (Google Merchant + OpenAI) to resolve
  the merchant outside `unstable_cache` so cache tags always use
  canonical merchant.id (UUID), not slugs. This fixes a cache
  invalidation blind spot where slug-based and UUID-based entries
  could diverge.

- Add `manage_stock` to OpenAI feed route's product select and type.
  When `manage_stock === false`, simple products now correctly emit
  `in_stock` with quantity 9999 (unlimited stock).

- Create shared `feed-identifier.ts` with `resolveFeedMerchant()` and
  `isUuid()` for reuse across feed routes and revalidation.

- Update revalidation endpoint to accept `{ merchant_id }` (preferred)
  alongside legacy `{ identifier }`. Response shape changes from
  `{ revalidated, identifier }` to `{ revalidated, merchant_id }`.

- Update backfill script to send `{ merchant_id }` instead of slug.

- Standardize both feed routes to use `createAnonClient()` singleton.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(feed): address CodeRabbit review findings

- Mock @/lib/cache-headers in feed route tests (worktree resolution)
- Add error body assertion to revalidation 500 test
- Add response.status assertion to OpenAI cache tag test
- Use path alias @/lib/feed-identifier in test import
- Preserve Supabase error cause in feed-identifier throw
- Add merchant_id positive-path test for OpenAI route

commit.message:
Work (#5)

* Revamp dashboard, clients and appointments UI; add Conversations & Automations pages; reorganize settings/system/sidebar

### Motivation
- Simplify and refocus several admin screens to surface operational actions and reduce clutter from deep analytics and rarely-used filters.
- Provide compact, action-oriented summaries and visualizations so teams can quickly spot issues (AI handoffs, cancellations, errors) and act.
- Introduce two new working screens for monitoring dialog flow (`conversations`) and automation scenarios (`automations`).
- Reorganize navigation and settings to separate core MVP actions from legacy/secondary functionality.

### Description
- Reworked the dashboard (`app/page.tsx`) to show AI-centric KPIs and small charts rendered with `recharts`, added automation summary cards, and simplified recent-activity rendering; replaced several legacy charts/components with compact area/bar charts and attention cards.
- Rebuilt the appointments view (`app/appointments/page.tsx`) to surface operational status cards, a status bar chart, and an inline simplified table instead of the previous full-featured table and funnel/heatmap visualization.
- Simplified the clients screen (`app/clients/page.tsx`) by removing a large set of advanced filters and campaign UI, replacing them with lightweight actionable filters, summary metric cards, and a concise client table with scenario tags and next-appointment info.
- Added `app/conversations/page.tsx` to monitor inbound dialog flow, channel distribution and AI vs human handling with chart and list views.
- Added `app/automations/page.tsx` to present scenario metrics, health indicators and simple controls for automation scenarios.
- Updated system and settings pages (`app/system/page.tsx`, `app/settings/page.tsx`) to reorganize sections, add anchors/IDs for intra-page navigation, and surface core controls (agent toggle, knowledge uploads, roles, notifications) with clearer labels.
- Updated the sidebar (`components/layout/Sidebar.tsx`) to reflect new navigation structure, introduce a small "Future / Legacy" section for less-used links, and tune item rendering behavior for anchor/hash links.
- Misc: removed several complex helper utilities and UI modules from these pages, introduced `formatDate`/`formatCurrency` usages, adjusted icons and color palettes, and added small UX text/content changes across pages.

### Testing
- Performed a local type-check with `tsc --noEmit`, which completed successfully.
- Built the application with `yarn build` and verified the production build step completed without errors.
- Ran linting (`yarn lint`) and unit test command (`yarn test`) where available; these commands completed without reported failures.

* Restore data-driven admin screens and navigation

* Adjust settings page layout

* Move auto-systems section to settings

commit.message:
Fix party expense log pagination and loading loops (#133)

* fix(pwa): stabilize party expense log pagination

Summary:
- extract party expense pagination into a dedicated library with
  imperative loaders, stable snapshots, and newest-first expense
  ordering
- replace the buggy hook implementation with a Suspense-friendly
  wrapper and preload the visible expense chunks from the party route
- add unit and Playwright coverage for chunk pagination, live updates,
  and newly created expenses returning to the top of the log

Rationale:
- the previous hook could trigger infinite update loops and was hard to
  reuse safely for preloading and Suspense integration
- moving the pagination state outside the hook makes the loading model
  predictable and gives us focused tests around chunk behavior

Tests:
- pnpm lingui:extract
- pnpm lint
- pnpm typecheck
- pnpm test
- pnpm --filter @trizum/pwa exec playwright test e2e/expense-log.spec.ts
  --project=chromium --workers=1 --reporter=line
- PLAYWRIGHT_BASE_URL=http://localhost:5173 pnpm --filter @trizum/pwa
  exec playwright test e2e/expense-log.spec.ts --project=chromium
  --workers=1 --reporter=line

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(pwa): stop resuspending the expense log after mount

Summary:
- remove the local ExpenseLog Suspense boundary from the party route
- keep already loaded expenses visible while new leading chunks load in
  the background
- make the expense pagination hook non-suspending and extend the unit
  coverage for leading-chunk updates

Rationale:
- once the party route has mounted we do not want new synced chunks to
  bounce the whole screen back into a pending state
- the route loader already preloads the visible expense page, so the
  hook can safely switch to background loading for follow-up updates

commit.message:
fix(feed): add missing stock_quantity and manage_stock to GMC feed query (#851)

The Google Merchant Center feed route was selecting only the legacy
`stock` column, ignoring `stock_quantity` and `manage_stock`. This
caused products with manage_stock=false to appear out-of-stock (instead
of unlimited/9999) and products using the modern stock_quantity field to
fall back to the legacy stock value (often 0).

Extracts the select projection into a testable constant in feed-query.ts
and adds route-level regression tests that capture the actual select
argument and assert both columns are present.

commit.message:
Harden release publish follow-up

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Extract observability into dashboard skill folders (#68) closes #60 ### Behavior Delta | | Before | After | |---|---|---| | Trigger | Observability code compiled only from `elixir/lib`, with terminal rendering, throughput helpers, and HTTP API source spread across the app tree. | Observability code compiles from dedicated `dashboard/*/lib` folders for terminal UI, metrics, and HTTP API concerns. | | Observable effect | No `dashboard/` skill folders existed, and the extraction target from GH-60 was not represented in the filesystem. | `dashboard/terminal-ui`, `dashboard/metrics`, and `dashboard/http-api` each exist with `SKILL.md` files while the terminal dashboard and API behavior stay the same. | | Affected inputs | Dashboard rendering, API routes, and TPS helpers depended on files under `elixir/lib/...`. | The same routes, modules, and dashboard entrypoints remain, but source files now load from the extracted dashboard folders through Mix compile paths. | ### Invariants / Non-goals - **Must still hold**: `SymphonyElixir.StatusDashboard`, `SymphonyElixirWeb.Router`, `SymphonyElixirWeb.Presenter`, and `SymphonyElixirWeb.ObservabilityApiController` keep their existing module names and behavior. - **Explicitly unchanged**: The shared observability pub/sub interface stays in `SymphonyElixirWeb.ObservabilityPubSub`, and the dashboard/API endpoints keep the same paths and payload contracts. - **Out of scope**: This PR does not redesign dashboard output, API schema, or LiveView behavior beyond relocating source files and extracting metrics helpers. ### Validation | Risk point | Evidence | Link | |---|---|---| | Terminal dashboard rendering regresses after file moves | `mix test test/symphony_elixir/status_dashboard_snapshot_test.exs test/symphony_elixir/orchestrator_status_test.exs` | N/A (local command) | | HTTP API/presenter wiring breaks after router/controller move | `mix test test/symphony_elixir/extensions_test.exs test/symphony_elixir/observability_pubsub_test.exs` | N/A (local command) | | Extraction breaks project-wide build/lint/type gates | `make -C elixir all` | N/A (local command) | ### Risk / Blast Radius / Rollback - **Most likely failure**: Mix compile-path wiring could miss one of the extracted directories, causing module load failures in dashboard or API paths. - **Blast radius**: Observability-only modules under the dashboard terminal UI, metrics, and HTTP API surfaces. - **How to detect**: Focused dashboard/API tests fail, or `make -C elixir all` fails during compile, coverage, or dialyzer. - **How to rollback**: Revert commit `6ad3d86` to restore the prior in-tree file locations. ### Review Focus 1. Confirm the extracted `dashboard/*/lib` compile paths in `mix.exs` are sufficient and do not change runtime behavior. 2. Check that the moved router/controller/presenter files preserve the existing API and LiveView entrypoints. 3. Verify the `SymphonyElixir.Dashboard.Metrics` extraction covers all throughput/sparkline logic previously hosted in `StatusDashboard`.
Architecture Trace ### Context (C4-L1) No L1 change - this is an internal observability refactor inside the Symphony orchestrator application. ### Container (C4-L2) No L2 deployment change - the same Elixir application still serves the dashboard UI and HTTP API. ### Component (C4-L3) ```mermaid flowchart LR Orchestrator --> StatusDashboard StatusDashboard --> Metrics StatusDashboard --> ObservabilityPubSub Router --> ObservabilityApiController ObservabilityApiController --> Presenter DashboardLive --> Presenter ``` ### Code Trace (C4-L4) - `SymphonyElixir.StatusDashboard` -> render loop / formatting facade -> `dashboard/terminal-ui/lib/symphony_elixir/status_dashboard.ex` - `SymphonyElixir.Dashboard.Metrics` -> rolling TPS / sparkline helpers -> `dashboard/metrics/lib/symphony_elixir/dashboard/metrics.ex` - `SymphonyElixirWeb.Router` -> observability routes -> `dashboard/http-api/lib/symphony_elixir_web/router.ex` - `SymphonyElixirWeb.ObservabilityApiController` -> JSON endpoints -> `dashboard/http-api/lib/symphony_elixir_web/controllers/observability_api_controller.ex` - `SymphonyElixirWeb.Presenter` -> API/dashboard payload shaping -> `dashboard/http-api/lib/symphony_elixir_web/presenter.ex` ### Decision Record - **Decision**: Compile observability source directly from the new `dashboard/*/lib` folders while keeping the existing module names stable. - **Alternatives considered**: Keep source under `elixir/lib` and add only wrapper folders, or rename modules into a new namespace during extraction. - **Trade-offs**: This keeps behavior and call sites stable, but requires explicit external compile-path wiring in `mix.exs`. - **Why chosen**: It satisfies GH-60's requested self-contained folder structure with the smallest possible blast radius on the running application. - **Implementation links**: `elixir/mix.exs`, `dashboard/terminal-ui/lib/symphony_elixir/status_dashboard.ex`, `dashboard/metrics/lib/symphony_elixir/dashboard/metrics.ex`, `dashboard/http-api/lib/symphony_elixir_web/router.ex`
--------- Co-authored-by: Codex Co-authored-by: Codex Co-authored-by: siaan-bot

Summary:

- future agents need reproducible project context in the repo before
  feature work starts

commit.message:
Split preloaded contexts into JSON assets

Refactor @fedify/vocab-runtime's preloaded JSON-LD contexts so
that each context lives in its own static JSON asset under
packages/vocab-runtime/src/contexts/.

Keep the exported preloadedContexts registry and its URL keys
unchanged while replacing the monolithic in-code definition with
static JSON imports.  This makes single-context updates easier to
review and reduces unrelated merge conflicts.

Fixes https://github.com/fedify-dev/fedify/issues/627

Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
🎨 Palette: Add ARIA labels to product card icon buttons (#838)

* 🎨 Palette: Add ARIA labels to product card icon buttons

* 💡 What: Added dynamic `aria-label` attributes to the Wishlist, Cart, and Color Navigation icon-only buttons.
* 🎯 Why: To improve screen reader accessibility by providing descriptive context for buttons that lack visible text, ensuring all users can navigate the product cards effectively.
* ♿ Accessibility: Addresses missing ARIA labels on multiple interactive elements, making them discoverable and usable for assistive technologies.
